meta.title: Chinese Thought Atlas | Statement relationship timeline
meta.description: A Chinese philosophy timeline grouped by people, with sourced statements as relationship nodes.
brand.title: CHINESE THOUGHT ATLAS
brand.subtitle: systems, statements & relations
status.research: A person is the heading for a system of positions; every point below is a philosophical position. Select a person or statement to see details and first-degree relations.
topbar.search: Search people, statements, tags, or domains
filters.title: Filters
timeline.focusHint: Zoom to expand statements; select a person for the full system or a statement for details and related views
timeline.relationsPending: Green means agreement, similarity, or expansion; red means disagreement, refutation, or contrast. A link does not necessarily establish direct historical influence.
legend.title: Node legend
legend.structuralNote: Every relation terminates at a statement; person links can only be aggregates of statement relations.
info.title: Phase 1 research boundary
info.body: The system uses a person–statement–relation architecture. A person is never represented by a single quotation; every statement has its own text, domains, tags, source locator, review state, and independent links.
info.inclusion: Inclusion requires a locatable textual, teaching, or practical body of thought discussed in reliable philosophical, intellectual-historical, or specialist scholarship.
info.quote: A popular quotation alone does not qualify a person when no stable text and scholarly discussion can be located.
info.disclaimer: The register contains 155 people, while the public timeline currently shows only 18 reviewed positions for six people and 14 reviewed position relations. Collection status for everyone else remains in the internal research queue; research directions and person taxonomy are no longer presented as positions. The atlas cannot replace primary texts or scholarly histories.
